heading: North Tumbulgum vs North Wagga Wagga — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Tumbulgum, New South Wales and North Wagga Wagga, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Wagga Wagga has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to North Tumbulgum (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Tumbulgum or North Wagga Wagga safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Tumbulgum has a flood risk score of 59/100, while North Wagga Wagga scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Tumbulgum scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. North Wagga Wagga scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Tumbulgum or North Wagga Wagga?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Wagga Wagga has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
